overallSummary: Fabio Giglietto maintains a presence on Mastodon, LinkedIn, and Bluesky, focusing on topics related to social media analysis, disinformation, and computational social science. LinkedIn has the highest follower count, but engagement varies across platforms. Overall, the content centers around research, publications, and job opportunities.

contentThemes.description: Sharing research findings, publications, and related academic work.
Discussions and opportunities related to computational methods in social science.
Content about detecting and analyzing disinformation campaigns.
Announcements for research positions and calls for applications.

recommendations: Increase posting frequency on all platforms to maintain engagement.
Tailor content to each platform's audience; for example, more professional content on LinkedIn and more community-focused content on Mastodon.
Engage more directly with followers by asking questions and responding to comments.
Use relevant hashtags to increase the visibility of posts.
Cross-promote content between platforms to reach a wider audience.

platformInsights.bluesky: Bluesky shows a good balance of research updates and opportunities, with a decent level of engagement (likes and reposts). Focus on continuing to build community through discussions and sharing relevant content.
platformInsights.mastodon: Mastodon engagement is lower compared to other platforms. Experiment with different content formats and posting times to see what resonates best with the audience. Focus on building community and engaging in discussions.
platformInsights.linkedin: LinkedIn has the largest follower base and highest engagement, suggesting it is the most effective platform for reaching a professional audience. Continue sharing research updates, job opportunities, and industry insights.

topPerformingContent.content: Excited to announce that our research team has been awarded a new grant to study information operations across social media platforms.

Now hiring: Two postdoctoral researchers to join our team working on computational methods for social media analysis. Background in data science, computational social science, or related fields preferred.
Just published our new paper on coordinated inauthentic behavior on social media platforms!
